title: Beware of spoilers on Internets
wikitext:
[]: Beware of spoilers on Internets             Quote Post by Jayfive » Mon Nov 19, 2012 8:27 am Just so you guys who are not entering in the Q sub-forum know, both tropes and wiki have been updated with 3.0 spoilerific info. Tropes is behind spoiler-proofing as normal, wiki has (at time of writing) a brief plot synopsis.

[]:             Quote Post by Chiral_Spiral » Mon Nov 19, 2012 9:37 am thanks for the heads up. tvtropes is usually a spoiler minefield no matter how diligent the writers are- sometimes even the mere presence of a trope can be a spoiler

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Mon Nov 19, 2012 10:09 am Evageeks is the safest place to be right now. Seriously, these forums are the only place where you'll find a thoroughly enforced spoiler policy, with judges, jury and executioners that would make Dredd jealous. You can't even scroll down safely when watching a preview video or trailer for Q without running the risk of being spoiled by youtube comments.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Mon Nov 19, 2012 11:06 am A.T. Fish wrote: Evageeks is the safest place to be right now.

[]:             Quote Post by Grand Duke of Yashima » Mon Nov 19, 2012 3:22 pm I got a big spoiler from J-List of all places. Peter Payne saw the film over the weekend and then promptly posted character designs on his blog and his email blast. Then he let out a major spoiler in his review. Unfortunately unless Khara can turn this film around fast on the video release I'm going to cave in by T-day.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Nov 19, 2012 3:45 pm Yes, I'm having to stay away from anime sites and discussion threads entirely. It appears the biggest problem with waiting for a BD release won't be my will power at all.
